name: Netherlands
intro: The Netherlands adopted agile ways of working earlier and more thoroughly than almost anywhere in Europe — ING's model reorganisation became a global case study — and Dutch employers now expect delivery professionals to hold the credentials that match. Scrum certifications are near-universal in Amsterdam's tech and banking scene; PMP anchors the infrastructure, energy and logistics programmes that a country below sea level runs permanently. English is the working language of Dutch project teams, making international online training a natural fit. Batches are scheduled live online across the whole year at hours built for Netherlands: a weekend Sat–Sun cohort and a weekday Wed–Thu cohort each week, all landing in comfortable local daytime; every upcoming date and its exact Netherlands timing is listed in the schedule below. The cohort is international, the cases are practical, and the format spares your working week. Afterwards you get exam application support, calibrated mock exams, and trainer access for doubts until you certify.

faq.q: How much does certification training cost in Netherlands?
faq.a: The course fee is US$1,295 for PMP and US$695 for CSM, covering the full live online weekend workshop, study materials, mock exams and exam application support. PMI's PMP exam fee (US$425 member / US$675 non-member) is paid separately to PMI; the CSM exam is included in the course fee.

faq.q: Are the classes live or recorded?
faq.a: Every batch is taught live by an instructor over two weekend days — not pre-recorded videos. You can ask questions in real time, work through exercises with the cohort, and reach the trainer afterwards for doubts.

faq.q: Is the certification recognised by employers in Netherlands?
faq.a: Yes. PMP (from PMI) and CSM (from Scrum Alliance) are global credentials recognised identically everywhere, including by employers and recruiters across Netherlands.
